title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
description: The system may be vulnerable to data injection attacks if user input is not properly validated or sanitized before being used in database queries or other operations. An attacker could inject malicious SQL code or other harmful data to manipulate the system's data or functionality.
attack_vector: User input directly used in database queries without proper sanitization or parameterized queries.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.
